title: Architecture Evaluation, Quality Attributes, and Decision Governance · Claim Audit: Failure Containment
summary: The capstone architecture diagram shows components but not quality scenarios, trust boundaries, failure containment, evolution, cost, or rejected alternatives. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capstone failure containment increment in evaluated architecture and decision governance packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Define failure domains, resource bounds, dependency timeouts, degraded modes, safe stop, recovery, and blast-radius evidence.
Analyze concrete quality-attribute scenarios and trade accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, cost, and simplicity explicitly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Failure Containment · Theory Failure Containment
steps.instruction:
theory case — A box diagram omits quality tradeoffs, trust and failure boundaries, cost, evolution, dissent, review triggers, and rejected alternatives. The theory variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Define failure domains, resource bounds, dependency timeouts, degraded modes, safe stop, recovery, and blast-radius evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Failure Containment contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Failure Containment · Theory Failure Containment.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define failure domains, resource bounds, dependency timeouts, degraded modes, safe stop, recovery, and blast-radius evidence. For Predict Failure Containment · Theory Failure Containment, keep Failure Containment connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Failure Containment condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: GOV.UK Service Manual discovery and alpha guidance — Controls problem-first discovery, affected-user and constraint research, measures of success, riskiest-assumption prototypes, and evidence-based stop decisions before production building.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A box diagram omits quality tradeoffs, trust and failure boundaries, cost, evolution, dissent, review triggers, and rejected alternatives. The worked example variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Define failure domains, resource bounds, dependency timeouts, degraded modes, safe stop, recovery, and blast-radius evidence. A defensible solution applies Failure Containment, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to evaluated architecture and decision governance packet.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-5
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
